theme: Set enzyme levels for target metabolic fluxes
title: Enzyme Staging for Target Flux
strategies: trivial: set every enzyme to e_max_i/2, ignoring the target entirely -- reproduces the checker's own baseline (~0.1)

greedy: invert the Michaelis-Menten law per reaction using the GIVEN nominal reference concentration x_ref (the steady state at a uniform e=1 profile), treating that concentration as fixed regardless of the target -- correct when the target is close to nominal, badly wrong wherever the target redistributes flux across a shared branch point (the actual concentration there swings far from x_ref, flipping some reactions between the saturated and limiting regime)

strong: derive the concentration EACH metabolite would have to sit at for the target flux vector itself to be self-consistent (via the tree's production/consumption balance), classify each reaction against its Km at that derived concentration, and invert the exact rate law there instead of at the stale reference -- an exchange of 'assume x is fixed' for 'solve for the x the target implies'
